properties.name: El Capitan Bridge
properties.blurb: The bridge over the Merced just east of El Capitan. Short flat walk with the wall straight overhead.

properties.name: Church Bowl & Indian Creek Trail
properties.blurb: Quiet picnic meadow under the Royal Arches with the Indian Canyon trail climbing out the back.

properties.name: El Capitan Meadow
properties.blurb: Pull off Northside Drive at the meadow and look up.

properties.name: The Ahwahnee
properties.blurb: You don't have to be a guest. The Great Lounge and the back lawn are open to anyone.

properties.name: Sentinel Bridge South Side
properties.blurb: Half Dome catches the last light from the south end of the bridge.

properties.name: Muir's Glacial Erratic
properties.blurb: The boulder John Muir used as evidence the valley was carved by ice. Sits in the Merced. Look for the large feldspar chunks.

properties.name: Housekeeping Camp Swimming
properties.blurb: Sandy beach on the Merced behind Housekeeping Camp. Shallow, slow, and shaded by afternoon.

properties.name: Cascade Picnic Area
properties.blurb: Tables along Cascade Creek just inside the Arch Rock entrance. Easy stop on the way in or out.

properties.name: Trailhead Parking at Curry Village
properties.blurb: The day-use lot at Curry Village. Closest paid parking for the Mist Trail and the Happy Isles trailhead.

properties.name: Yosemite Lodge
properties.blurb: One of the best home bases for moving around the Valley. The lot fills up last because cars have to do the full loop before they end up parking here.

properties.name: The Fen
properties.blurb: One of the most peaceful areas of Yosemite Valley. Good spot for birding.

properties.name: Degnan's Deli
properties.blurb: Build-your-own deli sandwiches in Yosemite Village. Decent and filling for $10 to $12. The everyday lunch option when you don't want to drive out of the Valley.

properties.name: Curry Village Pizza Deck
properties.blurb: Outdoor pizza deck at Curry Village. Once decent, now genuinely good. The Half Dome is the standout.

properties.name: McGurk Meadow
properties.blurb: A 3.7-mile round trip off Glacier Point Road. One of the best wildflower hikes in the park; the old sheepherder's cabin partway in belongs to John McGurk. Wildflowers peak June through August.

properties.name: Lyell Canyon
properties.blurb: An 8-mile trail through a broad subalpine canyon with only 200 feet of gain. Starts across from the Dog Lake parking area. The further you go, the fewer people you see. Tioga Road season only.

properties.name: May Lake
properties.blurb: The best moderate hike in the high country. 1.2 miles each way and 500 feet of gain to an alpine lake under Mount Hoffmann, with views in every direction from the shoreline. The grade is steady but not steep, which makes it the right pick for pushing kids past where a flat walk would have ended. Swimming on warm afternoons, granite slabs on the east shore. Tioga Road season only.

properties.name: Gaylor Lake
properties.blurb: Steep but short. The trail leaves the Tioga Pass entrance station at 9,945 ft and climbs a little over a mile to a ridge at 10,500 ft, then drops into the Gaylor Lakes basin: granite shores, the Cathedral Range across the south, and the ruins of an 1880s silver mine on the slope above. The first half hour earns the rest. Best in late summer, July and August, once the snow is off the upper switchbacks.
